---
title: "Week 1 — Data, evidence, and statistics"
toc: true
---

> **Status:** placeholder. Real content will be developed during the
> course build.

## Big question

What can data tell us, and what can't it tell us?

## Day-by-day

### Monday — concept

Direct instruction and worked examples introducing data, evidence, and
the role of statistics in making claims under uncertainty.

### Wednesday — micro-lab ticket

Short scaffolded task: identify cases, variables, and the
response/explanatory roles in a study description.

### Friday — quiz / application

Short quiz mirroring Wednesday's micro-lab on a new scenario, followed
by a headline-critique application.

## Primary source basis

OpenIntro IMS Ch 1 (data-hello).

## Supplemental source / custom needs

Health and life-science framing for examples; no ISLBS or custom
material required for week 1.

## Learning goals

Students should be able to:

- [ ] Identify cases and variables in a study description.
- [ ] Distinguish response from explanatory variables.
- [ ] Describe a statistical question in plain language.
- [ ] Recognize when a headline overstates the evidence.

## Assessment links

Links to the bank/manifests for this week will appear here once items
are built.

- Micro-lab ticket: TBD
- Friday quiz: TBD
- Homework: TBD
